managerStatus.claim: Lionel Scaloni continues as manager through the 2026 World Cup, contracted since Feb 2023.

formationCandidates.claim: Scaloni runs a fluid system, nominally 4-3-3/4-2-3-1 but dropping into a 4-4-2 two-banks-of-four out of possession; reports describe the World Cup opener vs Algeria starting in a 4-4-2.

sources.name: World Soccer Talk / Squawka tactical analysis

tacticalProfileCandidates.claim: Sources conflict on press intensity: one says Scaloni favors high pressing, another says Argentina is 'not inherently a high-press team' and plays at its own pace. Current press_intensity=62 may be roughly right but evidence is mixed.

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Cristian Romero suffered a knee injury in April 2026 (out for the rest of his club season) and has disciplinary issues (2 red cards); returned fit for the World Cup squad.
Lisandro Martinez tore his ACL in Feb 2025, missed ~10 months, returned Nov 2025; playing again for Man Utd but condition should be watched.

Lautaro Martinez and Julian Alvarez are in excellent club form (Serie A top scorer 17 goals; Atletico Madrid 20+ goal contributions including Champions League), suggesting their overall=69 may be an underestimate relative to other elite forwards.
Nico Paz (Como, 21yo) is described as Argentina's brightest emerging player, called up for his first World Cup; not present in the modeled top-player list.

nationalStrengthContext.claim: Argentina is unbeaten in its last 10 matches (27 goals scored, 2 conceded) and holds FIFA rank 1 as of 2026-06-11.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Scaloni typically uses his first substitution around the 60th-70th minute when comfortably ahead, then makes a batch of changes in the 70s-80s; uses all 5 slots when the game is settled. He rotates the starting XI rather than fielding a fixed XI, and manages Messi's minutes by condition rather than a fixed protective pattern (benched vs Chile, subbed at his own request at 78' vs Colombia, played full 90 vs Venezuela).

sources.name: ESPN match report / World Soccer Talk

recommendedCodexNextActions: Verify Nico Paz inclusion in seed roster
Re-check Lautaro/Alvarez overall vs current club form
Confirm Romero/Lisandro Martinez availability status flags

managerStatus.claim: Luis de la Fuente remains manager; announced the 26-man World Cup squad on 2026-05-25.

formationCandidates.claim: Base shape is 4-3-3 with build-up shifting to 3-2-5/2-3-5 in possession and a 4-4-2 press out of possession; current default_formation=4-3-3 matches.

tacticalProfileCandidates.claim: Multiple tactical analyses describe Spain's press intensity and defensive line height as both 'High', suggesting press_intensity=76 and defensive_line_height=68 may be slight underestimates relative to other top sides.

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Marc Cucurella transferred from Chelsea to Real Madrid on 2026-06-15 (~€55M+€5M, 6-year deal); seed data may still show Chelsea.
Nico Williams has dealt with pubalgia and a hamstring injury through the season; his World Cup participation was seriously in doubt as of squad announcement.
Lamine Yamal suffered a hamstring injury (2026-04-22), missed Barcelona's final 6 La Liga games, and was managed with limited minutes (15min sub appearance) in Spain's World Cup opener.
Pau Cubarsi is an undisputed Barcelona starter (26 starts, 94.6% pass completion) and described as a breakout-candidate of the tournament; overall=69 may be an underestimate.

sources.name: FC Barcelona official / Total Football Analysis

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: The modeled top-5 list (Yamal, Cucurella, Nico Williams, Cubarsi, Porro) omits Rodri (captain-level anchor, described as 'the spine of the team'), Pedri, and Mikel Oyarzabal (scored the Euro 2024 final winner, scoring again at this World Cup) -- all widely described as more central to the team than several listed players.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: De la Fuente tends to make changes later (e.g. first sub at 71' in a 0-0 draw vs Cape Verde, criticized as too slow) but moves earlier (halftime double-sub) when comfortably ahead (4-0 vs Saudi Arabia); uses all 5 slots when the match allows; manages Yamal's minutes deliberately post-injury (planned early withdrawals).

recommendedCodexNextActions: Update Cucurella club to Real Madrid
Add Rodri/Pedri/Oyarzabal to modeled roster priority
Flag Yamal/Nico Williams fitness uncertainty

managerStatus.claim: Thomas Tuchel remains manager; extended his contract through Euro 2028 in Feb 2026.

formationCandidates.claim: England's actual base shape is 4-2-3-1 (build-up 3-2-5) with a double pivot (Rice + Anderson) and Bellingham as the 10, not the seed's default 4-3-3; confirmed starting XI vs Croatia matches this.

tacticalProfileCandidates.claim: England maintained over 70% possession across all 8 qualifying matches, suggesting possession_style=62 is too low; press_intensity=78 appears roughly accurate (aggressive press/counter-press is described as core to Tuchel's plan).

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Marc Guehi transferred from Crystal Palace to Manchester City on 2026-01-19 (~£20m); seed may still show Crystal Palace.
Bukayo Saka has been playing through Achilles pain; Tuchel has confirmed managing his minutes deliberately at the World Cup.

Jude Bellingham plays the central no.10 role linking midfield and attack and is described as central to the attack, yet is absent from the modeled top-5 list; Declan Rice and Marcus Rashford are also starters not reflected.
Phil Foden, Cole Palmer, and Trent Alexander-Arnold were deliberately left out of the World Cup squad as part of a tactical rebuild; if present in the modeled seed they should be removed.

nationalStrengthContext.claim: England qualified undefeated and unscored-on (8-0-0, 21 goals, 0 conceded) -- the first European team to do so for this cycle.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Tuchel tends to make changes later (65-72 minutes) but in clusters of 2-3 at once, often paired with a tactical shape change (e.g. inverting a full-back, converting a player into an auxiliary anchor) rather than pure like-for-like; uses all 5 substitutions in essentially every match observed (qualifiers and World Cup opener).

recommendedCodexNextActions: Update default_formation to 4-2-3-1
Update Guehi club to Manchester City
Add Bellingham to modeled roster priority
Remove Foden/Palmer/Alexander-Arnold if present

managerStatus.claim: Didier Deschamps remains manager through the 2026 World Cup but publicly announced in Jan 2025 that he will step down afterward; Zinedine Zidane has reportedly verbally agreed to succeed him.

formationCandidates.claim: France's actual setup is a pragmatic mix of 4-2-3-1 and 4-3-3 with a double pivot protecting the back line, consistent with the seed's 4-2-3-1 default.

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Ousmane Dembele won the 2025 Ballon d'Or (ahead of Lamine Yamal) following a treble-winning season with PSG; current overall=77 appears clearly underrated relative to this status.

William Saliba has had a recurring back issue through the season but Deschamps has publicly denied it affects World Cup availability; overall=68 may be undervalued for a player rated among the world's top center-backs.

Michael Olise scored a hat-trick in a pre-World-Cup friendly and contributed a decisive performance in France's World Cup opener vs Senegal; described as a future Ballon d'Or contender, suggesting overall=75 has room to rise.
Aurelien Tchouameni and N'Golo Kante (the team's double-pivot midfield core) are absent from the modeled top-5 list, which is attack-heavy (3 right-wingers).

nationalStrengthContext.claim: France's FIFA rank rose from 3rd to 2nd as of 2026-06-22 after beating Senegal.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Deschamps is conservative with substitution timing when leading -- first change typically after 65-68 minutes, often two at once -- and prefers like-for-like positional swaps rather than shape changes, consistent with using all 5 slots in decisive wins (e.g. 5 used vs Iraq).

sources.name: ESPN / Sky Sports / Sports Mole match reports

recommendedCodexNextActions: Raise Dembele overall significantly (Ballon d'Or winner)
Update FIFA rank to 2
Consider adding Tchoumeni/Kante to modeled roster priority

managerStatus.claim: Roberto Martinez remains manager, having won the 2025 Nations League (beating Spain on penalties).

formationCandidates.claim: Portugal's base shape is 4-3-3 (matches seed) with variation to a compact 3-4-2-1 against stronger opposition.

sources.name: World Soccer Talk / The Football Analyst

tacticalProfileCandidates.claim: Qualifying possession averaged ~67% with 91.3% pass completion, and analyses describe the back line as deliberately high to support possession-building -- both suggest possession_style=66 and defensive_line_height=60 may be underestimates.

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Cristiano Ronaldo is the current captain and started Portugal's World Cup opener vs DR Congo at 41, becoming the oldest outfield starter in World Cup history; if absent from the modeled roster, this is a major omission.
Rafael Leao has been injury-prone through 2025-26 and was benched for Portugal's World Cup opener; the seed's framing of him as the top-rated, guaranteed starter (overall=72) does not match this.

Vitinha finished 3rd in the 2025 Ballon d'Or and was Champions League final Man of the Match; described as 'the heart of the midfield', suggesting overall=65 (below Leao's 72) is a significant underrating.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Martinez tends to make his first change at halftime when reacting to a deficit (e.g. 2025 NL semifinal vs Germany), but has been criticized as slow to react in drawn/stagnant matches (last change at 83' in a 1-1 draw vs DR Congo); generally uses ~4 of 5 substitution slots rather than all 5; manages Ronaldo by playing him full minutes regardless of form, while Leao is used as an impact substitute rather than guaranteed starter.

recommendedCodexNextActions: Verify Ronaldo's presence in modeled roster
Raise Vitinha overall significantly relative to Leao
Reconsider Leao as guaranteed starter given injury history

managerStatus.claim: Zlatko Dalic remains manager, continuing through World Cup qualifying and the tournament.

formationCandidates.claim: Croatia's base 4-3-3 is largely accurate, but the team switched to a 3-4-2-1 (back three) in the World Cup match against England.

tacticalProfileCandidates.claim: Qualifying possession averaged ~70% with several attacking metrics ranking top-5 in Europe, suggesting possession_style=68 may be an underestimate (closer to 80 per some analysis).

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Luka Modric remains active as captain in his 5th World Cup (moved to AC Milan in 2025-26); recovered from a cheekbone fracture in April 2026 to play at the World Cup. He does not appear in the modeled top-5 player list.

Several other established starters (Kovacic, Perisic, Kramaric, Musa, Livakovic) also appear absent from the modeled top-player list, which is weighted toward younger players (Gvardiol, Sutalo, Stanisic, Baturina, Sucic).

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Dalic makes his first substitution around halftime-to-60th-minute when behind (58' vs England) and tends toward like-for-like swaps rather than shape changes; Modric (age 40) is substituted relatively early (58' vs England) as an explicit fatigue-management measure even in competitive matches.

sources.name: ESPN / FIFA Match Centre / World Soccer Talk

recommendedCodexNextActions: Verify Modric, Kovacic, Perisic, Kramaric, Musa, Livakovic presence in modeled roster -- this may explain the existing 'thin squad depth' diagnostic flag
Raise possession_style

managerStatus.claim: Ronald Koeman remains manager (appointed June 2023), led the team through an unbeaten qualifying campaign.

formationCandidates.claim: Netherlands used a 4-2-3-1 in their World Cup win over Sweden (5-1), differing from the seed's default 4-3-3.

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Jurrien Timber is out of the World Cup with a groin injury (KNVB announced around 2026-06-09); replaced by Lutsharel Geertruida. Seed lists him as the 2nd-highest-rated player, which does not reflect his unavailability.
Donyell Malen transferred from Aston Villa to AS Roma in Jan 2026 and has been in excellent form there (league-leading scoring in the second half of the season).

Brian Brobbey, Frenkie de Jong, and Virgil van Dijk (captain) are absent from the modeled top-5 list; Brobbey scored twice off the bench vs Sweden as a breakout forward, de Jong and van Dijk are described as key players in tournament previews.

nationalStrengthContext.claim: FIFA rank reported as 7th (2026-04-01) and 8th (2026-06-11) by different sources -- current seed value of 7 may be one position stale.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Koeman makes his main substitution wave around 60-72 minutes, mixing like-for-like swaps with some role shifts (e.g. bringing on Depay to change the attacking focal point); when leading, the team shifts toward defensive/conservative substitutions which drew criticism after Netherlands conceded a late equalizer vs Japan after such a change.

recommendedCodexNextActions: Reflect Timber's injury-out status
Update Malen's club to AS Roma
Consider adding Brobbey/de Jong/van Dijk to modeled roster priority

managerStatus.claim: Javier Aguirre remains manager (appointed July 2024), his 3rd World Cup as Mexico coach.

formationCandidates.claim: Mexico's nominal 4-3-3 functions closer to a hybrid 4-2-3-1 in possession according to tactical analysis of the World Cup opener.

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Gerardo Arteaga was NOT selected for Mexico's official 26-man World Cup squad (multiple Spanish-language outlets report this and his own confusion about being left out); the seed lists him as a starting left-back.

Santiago Gimenez's club is AC Milan (not Feyenoord) and he has had a severely poor scoring season there (0 goals in 16 Serie A appearances) plus an ankle injury; this is not reflected if seed assumes good current form.
Cesar Montes's age in seed (27) does not match his actual age (29 per FotMob).

Gilberto Mora (17, Tijuana) is described as the tournament's biggest breakout prospect and youngest player; Raul Jimenez and captain Edson Alvarez are also described as central but are absent from the modeled top-5 list.

nationalStrengthContext.claim: As host nation, Mexico did not play qualifiers and instead scheduled an unusually heavy slate of high-profile friendlies (Portugal, Belgium, Serbia, Australia, Ghana) in 2025-26.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Aguirre makes substitutions late (typically 66+ minutes), mostly like-for-like, and uses all 5 slots in both confirmed World Cup matches; the Gimenez/Raul Jimenez striker swap has so far been a reactive in-game substitution (R. Jimenez starts, S. Gimenez/A. Gonzalez enters late) rather than a planned lineup rotation.

recommendedCodexNextActions: Remove or downgrade Arteaga (not selected for World Cup squad)
Update Gimenez club to AC Milan and reflect poor form/injury
Correct Montes age to 29
Consider adding Mora/Jimenez/Alvarez to modeled roster priority

managerStatus.claim: Carlo Ancelotti remains manager, appointed May 2025.

formationCandidates.claim: Ancelotti's Brazil primarily uses 4-3-3 (shifting to attacking 4-2-4 against deep blocks), not the seed's default 4-2-3-1.

sources.name: Tactical Football Analysis / World Soccer Talk

tacticalProfileCandidates.claim: Brazil's possession-oriented build-up (CBs splitting wide) suggests possession_style=68 could be slightly higher (70s); press_intensity=60 and defensive_line_height=58 are roughly consistent with Ancelotti's reactive, organized-but-not-high-press defensive approach.

sources.name: Tactical Football Analysis / The Football Analyst

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Raphinha suffered a hamstring injury in the World Cup match vs Haiti (2026-06-19), with reports of a minimum 7-10 day recovery, creating uncertainty for the rest of the tournament.

Neymar has returned to the World Cup squad and Estevao (Chelsea, a major young talent) is OUT of the squad due to a serious hamstring injury from April 2026 -- if the seed includes Estevao as available, this is incorrect; if it excludes Neymar, that is also incorrect.
Matheus Cunha scored twice in a recent World Cup match and is in good scoring form for Manchester United; overall=69 may be a slight underestimate.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Ancelotti is statistically a late substituter (average ~72nd minute at his most recent club), tends toward like-for-like swaps, and rarely uses all 5 slots (3+ subs used in ~69% of matches per available stats, not a full 5).

sources.name: Yahoo Sports (Ancelotti substitution stats) / Coaches' Voice

recommendedCodexNextActions: Update default_formation to 4-3-3
Reflect Raphinha injury uncertainty
Verify Neymar/Estevao roster status

managerStatus.claim: Mohamed Ouahbi is the actual current manager, but only since 2026-03-05, replacing Walid Regragui after the latter resigned; this is a very recent change worth flagging given how close it is to the tournament.

formationCandidates.claim: Morocco's setup is a flexible 4-3-3/4-2-3-1 under Ouahbi, consistent with the seed default.

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Nayef Aguerd was cut from Morocco's World Cup squad on 2026-06-11 due to a groin injury (replaced by Marwane Saadane) and has not played in the tournament; the seed lists him as a starting CB.
Achraf Hakimi is the team captain and described as one of the world's best full-backs; overall=68 may be an underestimate given his status as the team's clear leader.

Yassine Bounou (GK), Sofyan Amrabat (CM), and Ismael Saibari (who scored vs Brazil) are described as core players but are absent from the modeled top-3 list; En-Nesyri and Boufal were left off the final squad entirely.

sources.name: Squawka / Al Jazeera / Morocco World News

nationalStrengthContext.claim: Morocco is unbeaten in its last 10 matches (6 wins, 3 draws), tied for first in its World Cup group with Brazil.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: No specific quantitative substitution-timing data could be found for Ouahbi; qualitative reports suggest a pragmatic approach (sitting in a low block to protect a lead, as seen at the U-20 World Cup final he won before this appointment).

recommendedCodexNextActions: Remove/downgrade Aguerd (not in World Cup squad)
Consider adding Bounou/Amrabat/Saibari to modeled roster priority
Remove En-Nesyri/Boufal if present

managerStatus.claim: Rudi Garcia remains manager, appointed Jan 2025.

formationCandidates.claim: Garcia varies between 4-2-3-1, 4-3-3, and a back-three 3-4-2-1 depending on opponent (e.g. 3-4-2-1 vs Croatia); the seed default of 4-2-3-1 is a reasonable primary choice but not fixed.

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Kevin De Bruyne is absent from the modeled top-3 list despite being an active, selected World Cup squad member (moved to Napoli in 2025, suffered a serious hamstring tear in Oct 2025, returned to play in March 2026); the captaincy passed to Youri Tielemans in Sept 2025.
Koni De Winter transferred from Genoa to AC Milan on 2025-08-13 (5-year deal); seed may not reflect this club change.
Romelu Lukaku (striker), Youri Tielemans (current captain), and Thibaut Courtois (goalkeeper) are described as core squad members but absent from the modeled top-3 list.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: No specific quantitative substitution data found for Garcia; qualitative reports suggest an attacking-minded bench (Trossard, De Ketelaere, Fofana, Saelemaekers have scored as substitutes in qualifying).

recommendedCodexNextActions: Add Kevin De Bruyne to modeled roster (high priority, major omission)
Update De Winter club to AC Milan
Consider adding Lukaku/Tielemans/Courtois to modeled roster priority

managerStatus.claim: Julian Nagelsmann remains manager (since Sept 2023).

formationCandidates.claim: Germany's base 4-2-3-1 matches the seed; the team also shifts to 4-2-2-2 against deep blocks.

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Jamal Musiala suffered a severe fibula fracture and ankle injury/dislocation in mid-2025, missed ~196 days, but fully returned to play and was selected for and is playing in the World Cup as of the 26-man squad announced 2026-05-21; the seed's framing as an injury concern should be updated to reflect active participation.
Nico Schlotterbeck suffered an ankle ligament injury in Germany's 2nd World Cup match (vs Ivory Coast, 2026-06-20) and is ruled out for the rest of the tournament per DFB announcement.
Florian Wirtz transferred from Leverkusen to Liverpool in July 2025 (club-record fee); seed may not reflect this club change.

nationalStrengthContext.claim: FIFA rank confirmed at 10th as of June 2026, matching the seed value.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Nagelsmann uses halftime reviews to plan tactical/personnel changes for the second half, and is described as making reactive opponent-specific swaps (e.g. swapping a defensive midfielder for a more progressive one against high-press opponents); specific average substitution count/timing data is not available.

sources.name: Squawka / Bavarian Football Works / Get German Football News

recommendedCodexNextActions: Update Musiala to active/playing status (recovered)
Mark Schlotterbeck as unavailable for remainder of tournament
Update Wirtz club to Liverpool

managerStatus.claim: Marcelo Bielsa remains manager (since 2023, contracted through the World Cup).

formationCandidates.claim: Uruguay's 4-3-3 base (build-up via a 2+1 structure) matches the seed default.

sources.name: Tactical Football Analysis / The Football Analyst

tacticalProfileCandidates.claim: Multiple analyses describe Uruguay's press as 'relentless' and the defensive line as 'notoriously high', consistent with the seed's press_intensity=88 and defensive_line_height=74 (both already set very high).

sources.name: Tactical Football Analysis / The Football Analyst

tacticalProfileCandidates.claim: Uruguay held 67% possession (a national record at a World Cup) vs Saudi Arabia, suggesting possession_style=54 may be an underestimate.

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Ronald Araujo has a calf injury and has not played in either of Uruguay's first two World Cup matches; the seed lists him as a starting CB.
Darwin Nunez's current club is Al Hilal (Saudi Arabia), not any prior club the seed might assume, and he has been in poor recent form (substituted at halftime in the World Cup opener).
Federico Valverde (captain per some sources, vice-captain per others) is described as the team's most important player (8 goals + 8 assists across 2026) and is absent from the modeled top-3 list.

nationalStrengthContext.claim: FIFA rank as of the pre-tournament June 2026 update is 16, not the seed's 14.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Bielsa does not believe in 'plan B' substitutions and instead favors early, decisive changes when a player is underperforming -- confirmed by an immediate halftime substitution of a struggling Darwin Nunez in the World Cup opener, which directly preceded an attacking improvement.

sources.name: Modern Soccer Coach / Goal.com match report

recommendedCodexNextActions: Reflect Araujo's injury-out status
Update Nunez club to Al Hilal and reflect current poor form
Add Valverde to modeled roster (high priority, major omission)
Update FIFA rank to 16

managerStatus.claim: Nestor Lorenzo remains manager (since June 2022).

formationCandidates.claim: Colombia varies between 4-2-3-1 (matches seed default) and 4-3-3.

tacticalProfileCandidates.claim: Analysis describes Lorenzo's identity as prioritizing balance and tactical discipline over possession dominance, suggesting possession_style=60 may be slightly too high (closer to 50).

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Luis Diaz transferred from Liverpool to Bayern Munich in summer 2025 (~€75M) and has scored 15 goals/13 assists in 28 Bundesliga matches; overall=73 appears significantly underrated for this level of production.
James Rodriguez remains team captain and the creative focal point (most assists in CONMEBOL qualifying) despite an unstable club situation (multiple moves); he is absent from the modeled top-3 list.
Daniel Munoz and Davinson Sanchez are both established regulars at title-winning clubs (Crystal Palace, Galatasaray respectively); their overalls (63, 60) may be modest underestimates.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Lorenzo is conservative with substitutions, e.g. using only 4 of 7 allowed in a friendly vs Brazil, and tends toward late defensive changes to protect a result.

recommendedCodexNextActions: Raise Luis Diaz overall significantly
Add James Rodriguez to modeled roster (high priority, captain/creative core)

managerStatus.claim: Mauricio Pochettino remains manager, named the official 26-man World Cup roster.

formationCandidates.claim: The USA's actual base formation in the World Cup opener vs Paraguay was 4-2-3-1, not the seed's 3-4-2-1; Pochettino has used multiple back-line structures but 4-2-3-1 is the more consistent recent baseline.

tacticalProfileCandidates.claim: Measured possession over the prior 12 months averaged ~54.8%, closely matching the seed's possession_style=58.

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Antonee Robinson has had a chronic knee issue since Oct 2024 requiring May 2025 surgery, missing most of the 2025-26 club season and returning gradually (only one full 90 minutes all season); seed's framing as a fit regular starter may be optimistic.
Christian Pulisic suffered a calf injury during the World Cup opener and missed the following match (vs Australia) before returning to training.
Alex Freeman (not in modeled top-3) has played every minute of the World Cup so far and scored against Australia, suggesting he is a more central player than the current top-3 list implies.

nationalStrengthContext.claim: FIFA rank confirmed at 17 as of 2026-06-11, matching the seed.

As host nation the USA's pre-tournament form was uneven against top opposition (losses to Belgium 2-5, Portugal 0-2, Germany 1-2) despite wins over lesser opposition -- suggests the team's true strength against elite teams may be lower than its overall record implies.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Pochettino has made limited rotation so far in the tournament (several players have played every minute), and made an explicitly risk-averse halftime substitution of Pulisic in the opener rather than risk aggravating a minor issue.

recommendedCodexNextActions: Update default_formation to 4-2-3-1
Flag Robinson's reduced fitness/form this season
Consider adding Alex Freeman to modeled roster priority

managerStatus.claim: Hajime Moriyasu remains manager (since 2018), his 2nd World Cup in charge.

formationCandidates.claim: Japan's actual starting formation vs Netherlands (World Cup, 2026-06-14) was 3-4-2-1, not the seed's 4-2-3-1; Moriyasu is described as flexible but 3-4-2-1 is now the more consistent recent baseline.

sources.name: World Soccer Talk / OneFootball confirmed lineups

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Takehiro Tomiyasu had his Arsenal contract terminated in July 2025 (recurring knee surgeries, almost no playing time) and joined Ajax on a short-term deal, called up to the World Cup squad as a late injury replacement; seed's club (Arsenal) and overall=61 as a trusted starter may both be stale/optimistic.
Ritsu Doan moved to Eintracht Frankfurt (Aug 2025) and has captained the national team in multiple recent matches including the World Cup opener, suggesting his role/importance exceeds overall=62.
Kaoru Mitoma (Brighton, one of Japan's best players) missed selection for the World Cup squad due to a hamstring injury; should not be modeled as available if the seed includes him as a top player.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Moriyasu is known for a 'super-sub' strategy, deliberately holding back attacking depth to exploit tired opponents late; used all 5 substitution slots in the World Cup opener vs Netherlands, with a substitute (Ogawa) scoring the late equalizer.

sources.name: Tactical Football Analysis / ESPN match report

recommendedCodexNextActions: Update default_formation to 3-4-2-1
Update Tomiyasu club to Ajax and reconsider his overall after long injury layoff
Verify Mitoma's exclusion from the World Cup squad is reflected

managerStatus.claim: Pape Thiaw remains manager (appointed 2024-12-13), leading Senegal through AFCON 2025 and the 2026 World Cup.

formationCandidates.claim: Base shape is 4-3-3 with Idrissa Gueye as a single pivot, but Thiaw varies to 3-4-2-1 (defensive reinforcement) or 4-2-4 when chasing a game; matches the seed default.

tacticalProfileCandidates.claim: Qualitative reporting describes selective wave-based pressing, vertical/direct play over sustained possession, and a higher back line exploited behind advancing full-backs -- directionally consistent with press_intensity=74/possession_style=48/defensive_line_height=58, but no numeric benchmark was found to confirm the exact values.

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: El Hadji Malick Diouf transferred from Slavia Prague to West Ham United on 2025-07-15 (~£19m); seed may still show his prior club.
Nicolas Jackson went on a season-long loan from Chelsea to Bayern Munich (2025-09-01, won Bundesliga) and is expected back at Chelsea after the loan; seed club should reflect the loan status.

Kalidou Koulibaly (captain, Al-Hilal) suffered two thigh muscle tears plus a hematoma in training just before the World Cup and was named to the provisional squad despite the injury -- significant availability risk not reflected if seed shows him fully fit.

Ismaila Sarr (Crystal Palace) won the 2025-26 UEFA Conference League top-scorer and Player of the Season awards and scored twice in Senegal's World Cup match vs Norway; current overall=64 may be an underestimate of his present form.

Sadio Mane (Al Nassr, 34) and the injured-but-selected captain Koulibaly are not among the seed's listed top-3 modeled players for Senegal, despite Mane being described as the side's standout performer at AFCON 2025.

nationalStrengthContext.claim: Senegal qualified for the World Cup unbeaten in CAF qualifying (7W-3D, 24 points), finishing top of its group.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Thiaw tends to hold tactical adjustments until the second half, shifting to 3-4-2-1/4-2-4 when behind and to a back-five (5-4-1) when protecting a lead, suggesting system-change substitutions rather than pure like-for-like; no quantified timing/count data was found.

recommendedCodexNextActions: Update Diouf club to West Ham United
Update Jackson club to reflect Bayern loan / Chelsea ownership
Flag Koulibaly injury risk despite squad selection
Re-check Sarr overall given current form
Consider adding Mane to modeled roster priority

managerStatus.claim: Murat Yakin remains manager (since Aug 2021), contracted through the 2026 World Cup.

formationCandidates.claim: 4-3-3 is Yakin's primary base shape (matches seed), but he switches to a back-three 3-4-3 against stronger opposition or in knockout-stage contexts.

tacticalProfileCandidates.claim: Analysis describes a Xhaka-anchored compact central block with possession emphasis, but no numeric benchmark for press_intensity=60/possession_style=66/defensive_line_height=55 was found; these vary heavily by opponent (e.g. a deeper 5-4-1 against stronger sides).

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Manuel Akanji moved on a one-year loan from Manchester City to Inter on 2025-09-01 (2026 buy option); seed should reflect Inter rather than Manchester City.

Dan Ndoye transferred from Bologna to Nottingham Forest on 2025-07-31 (5-year deal, ~$46m+); his 2025-26 club scoring output has been modest (1 goal, 1 assist in league play) relative to seed overall=66.
Granit Xhaka (captain, Sunderland) and Breel Embolo are described as central to the team's structure and attack but are absent from the seed's top-3 modeled player list, which lists only defenders.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Yakin made a double substitution in the 72nd minute against Bosnia at the World Cup (Manzambi + Vargas) that directly produced 3 of the team's 4 goals, described in coverage as 'golden substitutions' -- a pattern of using simultaneous attacking double-changes to change games, rather than single like-for-like swaps.

recommendedCodexNextActions: Update Akanji club to Inter (loan)
Update Ndoye club to Nottingham Forest
Consider adding Xhaka/Embolo to modeled roster priority

managerStatus.claim: Amir Ghalenoei remains manager (2nd stint, since 2023), leading Iran through World Cup qualifying and the 2026 World Cup.

formationCandidates.claim: 4-2-3-1 is the primary qualifying/World Cup shape (matches seed), but Ghalenoei is pragmatic and switches to 4-4-2 or a deeper 5-4-1 depending on the opponent.

tacticalProfileCandidates.claim: Analysts consistently describe Iran as a deep, compact, low-possession, counter-reliant side that protects the box rather than presses high -- directionally supporting low defensive_line_height=28/possession_style=35/press_intensity=42, but no numeric benchmark confirms the exact values.

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Mehdi Taremi (now at Olympiacos after Inter and FC Porto, with a Champions League final appearance) has a current overall=53 that appears clearly underrated relative to his Serie A/Primeira Liga career and ~60 international goals; he had 10 goals and 7 assists in World Cup qualifying.
Hossein Kanaanizadegan remains at Persepolis as the experienced defensive anchor; this matches the seed's framing and overall=52 looks reasonable for his role.

nationalStrengthContext.claim: FIFA rank confirmed at 20 as of 2026-06-11, matching the seed.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Several Iran substitutions against New Zealand at the World Cup were explicitly attributed by Ghalenoei to player cramping/fitness issues from travel rather than tactical planning -- a condition-driven substitution pattern rather than a fixed tactical timing.

recommendedCodexNextActions: Raise Taremi overall substantially given career level
Update Taremi club to Olympiacos
Mehdi Ghaedi/Ghayedi's current club (Al-Nasr vs Shabab Al-Ahli) is unresolved in research -- keep as review question only

managerStatus.claim: Vincenzo Montella remains manager; the Turkish Football Federation confirmed he is retained despite a group-stage World Cup exit.

formationCandidates.claim: 4-2-3-1 is confirmed as Montella's base shape (matches seed), shifting to 4-1-3-2 or 3-4-2-1 in defensive phases.

tacticalProfileCandidates.claim: Turkey dominated possession in both World Cup group games (e.g. 32 shots to 7 vs Paraguay), suggesting possession_style=56 is an underestimate; press_intensity=64 may be slightly high since the press is described as selective/trap-based rather than constant.

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Kenan Yildiz (Juventus) won Serie A's 2025-26 Rising Star award with 11 goals/9 assists across all competitions; overall=68 may be a modest underestimate.

Arda Guler (Real Madrid) won UEFA's 'Revelation of the Season 2025/26' for the Champions League and had 4 goals/8 assists in 31 league games at 90% pass completion; overall=66 appears clearly underrated.
Merih Demiral extended his contract with Al-Ahli (Saudi Arabia) through 2029, confirming his current club as Al-Ahli rather than any prior club still in the seed.

nationalStrengthContext.claim: Turkey qualified for the 2026 World Cup via the UEFA playoffs (beating Romania 1-0 then Kosovo 1-0 on 2026-03-31), its first World Cup appearance since 2002, then was eliminated in the group stage (losses to Australia 0-2 and Paraguay 0-1).

sources.name: Wikipedia, 2026 FIFA World Cup Group D

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: In both World Cup group matches Montella used all 5 substitution slots, with attacking double-changes around the 60th minute and further adjustments in the 70s-80s -- a mix of like-for-like and goal-chasing tactical changes.

sources.name: Wikipedia, 2026 FIFA World Cup Group D

recommendedCodexNextActions: Confirm Turkey is modeled as a 2026 World Cup participant (qualified via playoff, eliminated in group stage)
Raise Yildiz/Guler overall, especially Guler
Update Demiral club to Al-Ahli

managerStatus.claim: Sebastian Beccacece remains manager (since Aug 2024) through World Cup qualifying and the 2026 World Cup.

formationCandidates.claim: Sources disagree between 4-4-2 (matches seed) and 4-2-3-1 for Ecuador's recent base shape; the predicted/observed World Cup-cycle starting XI more often resembles a 4-2-3-1, so the seed's 4-4-2 default is uncertain rather than confirmed.

sources.name: World Soccer Talk WC2026 Ecuador preview

tacticalProfileCandidates.claim: Reporting consistently describes relentless/aggressive pressing, quick direct transitions over sustained possession, and a compact defensive shape -- directionally supporting press_intensity=70/possession_style=40/defensive_line_height=35, with no numeric benchmark found to confirm exact values.

sources.name: World Soccer Talk WC2026 Ecuador preview

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Moises Caicedo (Chelsea, contract extended to 2033) has a current overall=62 that is dramatically underrated -- independent rating sources place his base overall around 87, and he is consistently described as one of the world's best defensive midfielders; he should rank above teammates Pacho (69) and Hincapie (67), not below them.

Willian Pacho plays for Paris Saint-Germain (2025 Champions League winner) and missed several matches in May 2026 with a thigh issue but was selected for the World Cup squad; seed club/availability should be checked against PSG.

Piero Hincapie is on loan at Arsenal from Bayer Leverkusen (2025 summer, buy option ~EUR52m) and has had recurring groin/shoulder issues through 2025-26; seed club/availability should reflect Arsenal and injury risk.

nationalStrengthContext.claim: Ecuador finished 2nd in CONMEBOL World Cup qualifying (behind Argentina) with only 5 goals conceded in 18 matches, the fewest in the group.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: When chasing a result Beccacece tends to change formation shape and add attacking players (Plata, Minda, Yeboah) rather than make purely like-for-like swaps; no quantified timing data was found.

sources.name: World Soccer Talk WC2026 Ecuador coverage

recommendedCodexNextActions: Raise Caicedo overall substantially -- he should be the team's top-rated player, not its lowest of the top-3
Update Pacho club to PSG and flag recent thigh issue
Update Hincapie club to Arsenal (loan) and flag recurring injury history

managerStatus.claim: Hong Myung-bo remains manager (since Jul 2024), having led Korea through World Cup qualifying unbeaten.

formationCandidates.claim: Korea shifted from a back-four during qualifying to a back-three; both confirmed World Cup starting XIs (vs Czechia and vs Mexico) used 3-4-2-1, not the seed's default 4-2-3-1.

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Son Heung-min transferred from Tottenham to LAFC (MLS) on 2025-08-06 (~$26.5m, MLS record fee) but remains national team captain and starting forward; seed club should reflect LAFC.

Kim Min-jae (Bayern Munich) has been pushed down the pecking order behind Tah/Upamecano this season and had a knee concern clear in May 2026 before being confirmed for the World Cup; he was also linked to the conceding action in Korea's 0-1 loss to Mexico.

Lee Kang-in plays for Paris Saint-Germain (squad rotation/backup minutes in 2025-26, ~1,500 league minutes) but remains Korea's primary creative midfielder, completing 100% of his passes in the World Cup opener vs Czechia.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: In both World Cup matches Hong made his first double substitution around the 57th-62nd minute, consistently withdrawing Son Heung-min for fresh attackers (Hwang Hee-chan, Oh Hyeon-gyu) before further changes in the 70s-80s.

recommendedCodexNextActions: Update default_formation to 3-4-2-1
Update Son club to LAFC
Flag Kim Min-jae's reduced role at Bayern and recent knee concern
Update Lee Kang-in club to PSG

managerStatus.claim: Ralf Rangnick remains manager (since Jun 2022), having led Austria to its first World Cup since 1998.

formationCandidates.claim: 4-2-3-1 is the base/build-up shape (matches seed), shifting to a 4-4-2 block out of possession and occasionally a 3-1-5-1 against two-striker opponents.

tacticalProfileCandidates.claim: Austria recorded the lowest PPDA (~7.1, i.e. the most intense press) of any European qualifier for this World Cup, consistent with Rangnick's 'Gegenpressing' identity; this suggests press_intensity=84 may be a slight underestimate relative to its status as the most pressing-intensive European side.

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Kevin Danso transferred from RC Lens to Tottenham Hotspur in Feb 2025 (completed permanently in 2025-26); seed club should reflect Tottenham.
Stefan Posch moved from Bologna to Como (2025-09, then permanent 2026-01) and is now on loan at Mainz 05 (from 2026-01-22); seed club is likely stale regardless of which prior club it shows.

Several attacking/midfield players described as central to the team (Christoph Baumgartner, Marko Arnautovic, Paul Wanner, Marcel Sabitzer, Konrad Laimer) are absent from the seed's top-3 modeled list, which contains only center-backs and a right-back.

nationalStrengthContext.claim: Austria lost only 1 of 8 World Cup qualifying matches (22 goals scored, 4 conceded) and beat Jordan 3-1 at the World Cup for its first World Cup win in 36 years, before losing 0-2 to Argentina.

recommendedCodexNextActions: Update Danso club to Tottenham
Update Posch club to Mainz 05 (loan)
Consider adding Baumgartner/Arnautovic to modeled roster priority
Consider raising press_intensity given league-low PPDA

managerStatus.claim: Graham Potter took over as manager on 2025-10-20 (replacing John Dahl Tomasson after a 0-1 loss to Kosovo) and had his contract extended through 2030 on 2026-03-12.

formationCandidates.claim: Sweden's World Cup starting XIs have consistently fielded Gyokeres and Isak together as a strike partnership (a two-forward system), reported variably as 3-5-2/4-3-3/4-2-3-1 depending on the source, which conflicts with the seed's single-striker 3-4-3 default.

sources.name: Wikipedia, 2026 FIFA World Cup Group F

keyPlayerStatusCandidates.claim: Viktor Gyokeres remains at Arsenal (joined summer 2025) and is in strong scoring form across competitions; seed overall=67 as the top-rated player appears consistent with his current status.

Anthony Elanga transferred from Nottingham Forest to Newcastle United in Jul 2025; seed club should reflect Newcastle. In the World Cup match vs Netherlands he came on as a 55th-minute substitute rather than starting, scoring 4 minutes later -- suggesting his role may be more impact-substitute than guaranteed starter.

Isak Hien (Atalanta) suffered a thigh injury against Ukraine in the March 2026 playoff semifinal that forced him out of the playoff final; he recovered in time to start the World Cup opener but carries elevated injury risk.

nationalStrengthContext.claim: Sweden finished its qualifying group winless and last under Tomasson, then reached the World Cup via the playoffs (beating Ukraine 3-1, Poland 3-2) under Potter; at the World Cup it beat Tunisia 5-1 but lost 1-5 to the Netherlands.

substitutionTendencyCandidates.claim: Trailing 0-3 to the Netherlands, Potter made a double substitution at the 55th-56th minute (Elanga, Bergvall, Zeneli all on within 2 minutes) -- an attacking, game-state-driven cluster of changes rather than like-for-like; used all 5 slots in that match. Based on a single sampled match, so the pattern is not yet confirmed as a general tendency.

recommendedCodexNextActions: Reconsider default_formation given the consistent Gyokeres+Isak two-forward system
Update Elanga club to Newcastle United and flag possible impact-substitute role rather than guaranteed starter
Flag Hien's recent hamstring/thigh injury history

crossTeamPatterns.pattern: Top-5-by-overall lists systematically omit anchor/creator-type midfielders across 6 of 16 teams researched (Rodri/Pedri for ESP, Bellingham for ENG, Tchoumeni/Kante for FRA, de Jong/van Dijk for NED, Mora/Alvarez for MEX, James Rodriguez for COL), even though independent sources describe these players as central. May indicate OVERALL_WEIGHTS' MID-group shooting/passing split underweights pure progression/control players rather than being a pure data-entry gap.

press_intensity/possession_style/defensive_line_height read low relative to independent tactical analysis for multiple top sides (ESP, ENG, POR, CRO, NED, URU all flagged), suggesting a possible systematic under-calibration for tactically sophisticated/elite teams.

Club-transfer and injury-status staleness recurs broadly (Cucurella/ESP, Guehi/ENG, Malen/NED, Gimenez/MEX, De Winter/BEL, Wirtz/GER, Luis Diaz/COL, Tomiyasu/JPN, Diouf+Jackson/SEN, Akanji+Ndoye/SUI, Taremi/IRN, Demiral/TUR, Pacho+Hincapie/ECU, Son+Lee Kang-in/KOR, Danso+Posch/AUT, Elanga/SWE all changed clubs without apparent seed reflection; Timber/NED, Araujo/URU, Aguerd/MAR, Schlotterbeck/GER, Mitoma/JPN, Koulibaly/SEN, Hien/SWE are all unavailable or injury-flagged for the World Cup but may be modeled as fully fit). Suggests the freshnessPolicy in metadata.json is not being operationally kept current; this is now confirmed across 24 of 24 researched teams.

default_formation mismatches between the seed and the team's actual recent/World-Cup starting shape recur in at least 7 of 24 researched teams (ENG: 4-3-3 seed vs actual 4-2-3-1; JPN: 4-2-3-1 seed vs actual 3-4-2-1; KOR: 4-2-3-1 seed vs confirmed 3-4-2-1 in both World Cup matches; SWE: 3-4-3 seed vs an actual two-forward Gyokeres+Isak system; SEN/SUI/AUT seeds are directionally correct but omit the manager's situational back-three/back-four variation). Recommend treating default_formation as the single most actionable existing-field correction category from this research pass.

At least 5 teams (POR, CRO, BEL, URU, COL) appear to be missing a single very famous, currently-active veteran or star player entirely from their modeled roster/top-player list (Ronaldo/POR, Modric/CRO, De Bruyne/BEL, Valverde/URU, James Rodriguez/COL). This is a distinct and higher-severity issue than overall-value miscalibration -- it suggests possible roster completeness gaps for elder statesmen players specifically.

futureEngineFeatureCandidates.claim: The simulation engine's substitution logic (backend/app/engine/management.py, maybe_substitute) is fatigue-only and identical for all 48 teams -- there is no per-manager substitution-tendency parameter. Real-world research across 8 managers found clear, distinguishable patterns (e.g. Bielsa makes early decisive halftime swaps; Ancelotti/Deschamps are late and conservative; Tuchel/Moriyasu use all 5 slots with tactical-shape changes; Dalic substitutes the 40-year-old Modric early as explicit fatigue management). This is a credible feature candidate for a future Codex spec, not a data field that exists today.
